title: Phycoschoenus isoetifolia
taxonomy: Phycoschoenus isoetifolia is classified as a species of sedge in the family Cyperaceae, which is commonly known as the sedge family. This plant is native to Australia and is often found in wetland habitats. From a botanical standpoint, Phycoschoenus isoetifolia is a member of the genus Phycoschoenus, which is a group of sedges that are typically found in aquatic environments. The species is further classified as a flowering plant, with its classification being Kingdom: Plantae, Division: Angiospermophyta, Class: Liliopsida, Order: Poales, Family: Cyperaceae, Genus: Phycoschoenus, and Species: P. isoetifolia. The classification of Phycoschoenus isoetifolia reflects its unique characteristics and adaptations to its native Australian habitat.
